details: The "Police Internship Scheme" was launched by the Home Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh. The scheme aims to familiarize meritorious students with the functioning of the Madhya Pradesh Police Department and to encourage research in policing and law enforcement.
Selected students are offered a six-week internship opportunity at district police stations, where they gain practical experience in administrative, legal, and investigative procedures.
benefits: Interns gain firsthand experience of police administration, law enforcement, investigation procedures, and public dealing.
Each intern submits an analytical research paper of 3,000 words on an approved topic related to policing.
A Police Internship Certificate is awarded upon successful completion by the respective unit head (Superintendent of Police).
Each intern is placed under the supervision of at least a Sub-Inspector or higher-ranking officer, who mentors and evaluates daily work.
Interns maintain a daily duty record, authenticated by their supervising officer the following day.
Large police stations (in Indore, Bhopal, Jabalpur, and Gwalior): 2–3 interns
Mode: Full-time, in-person at designated police stations.
The scheme is unpaid. No salary or stipend is provided. Lodging and food arrangements must be made by the interns themselves.
No leave is permitted during the internship period. Lack of interest or misconduct may result in termination by the Superintendent of Police.
eligibility: The applicant should be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.
The applicant should be a student enrolled in a recognized educational institution.
The applicant must be between 21 and 26 years of age as of May 1 of the application year.
The applicant should be pursuing one of the following courses:
Master’s or Postgraduate Program from any UGC-recognized institution
Diploma in Police-Related Subjects (e.g., Human Rights, Criminology)
Ph.D. or Research in Police-Related Subjects

offline: The District Superintendent of Police (SP) contacts major universities and colleges to invite applications.
The interested applicant downloads the application form (Annexure K) from the official website.
In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the respective District Superintendent of Police (SP) Office.
Applications are reviewed based on eligibility, academic merit, and interest in policing.
Qualified students are allotted police stations according to training capacity: 2–3 interns for large stations, 1 for medium stations.
Interns begin work on June 1, with supervision by designated officers.
Interns maintain daily duty logs and submit a 3,000-word analytical research paper.
Successful interns receive a Police Internship Certificate signed by the unit head.
Each district submits a list of selected interns by June 15 and completion details by August 1 to the Police Training Headquarters.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for the Police Internship Scheme?
faqs.answer: Students aged 21–26 pursuing law, engineering, management, computer, or police-related studies.

faqs.question: What is the duration of the internship?
faqs.answer: The internship duration is six weeks.

faqs.question: Where will the internship take place?
faqs.answer: At district police stations across Madhya Pradesh.

faqs.question: Is there any application fee?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme is completely free of charge.

faqs.question: Will interns receive any stipend or allowance?
faqs.answer: No, the internship is unpaid. Interns must bear their own lodging and food expenses.

faqs.question: How are students selected?
faqs.answer: Based on eligibility, academic merit, and interest in police studies.

faqs.question: Who supervises the interns?
faqs.answer: A police officer not below the rank of Sub-Inspector.

faqs.question: Can interns take leave during the internship?
Is any research component involved?
faqs.answer: Yes, interns must submit a 3,000-word analytical research paper.

faqs.question: Will participants receive any certificate?
faqs.answer: Yes, successful interns receive a Police Internship Certificate.

faqs.question: Can a student be removed from the program?
faqs.answer: Yes, for lack of interest or violation of discipline.

faqs.question: Who implements the scheme?
faqs.answer: The Superintendent of Police (SP) of each district under the Police Training Headquarters.

faqs.question: Who owns the rights to the research work?
faqs.answer: The Police Department retains publication rights; interns may use the work with prior permission from the SP.
